title: Hybrid Sequence Mixer Audit
research_question: Does every layer really need full attention, or can a hybrid stack keep most of the quality while using cheaper sequence mixers in selected layers?

candidates.name: j1-middle3
candidates.goal: Test the smallest middle-of-stack swap from attention into a cheaper sequence mixer.
candidates.hypothesis: The middle of the stack may be the safest place to use a cheaper sequence mixer while preserving input formation and output refinement.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the hybrid idea is viable at all.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the lowest-risk true architecture replacement, so it is the right first gate for the whole family.
candidates.description: replace the middle three attention layers with sequence mixers

candidates.name: j2-middle5
candidates.goal: Scale the hybrid idea beyond a minimal swap.
candidates.hypothesis: If the hybrid idea is real, more of the middle stack may be replaceable than we expect.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the win, if any, scales beyond a light touch.
candidates.why_worthy: This separates 'hybrid works a little' from 'hybrid changes the stack budget meaningfully'.
candidates.description: replace the middle five attention layers with sequence mixers

candidates.name: j3-alternating
candidates.goal: Test whether interleaving attention and mixers is better than block replacement.
candidates.hypothesis: Alternating global routing and cheaper local mixing may work better than separating them into contiguous regions.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the architecture wants layer-to-layer cooperation rather than region specialization.
candidates.why_worthy: This is a distinct structural theory, not just 'more mixer layers'.
candidates.description: alternate attention and sequence-mixer layers through the stack

candidates.name: j4-lower4
candidates.goal: Probe whether early layers can be made cheaper while later attention stays full-fidelity.
candidates.hypothesis: Lower layers may tolerate sequence mixers better than upper layers because later attention carries the final refinement burden.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether lower-layer replacement is the cleaner placement.
candidates.why_worthy: This directly mirrors the placement logic that mattered in tranche I, but for a completely different mechanism.
candidates.description: replace the lower four attention layers with sequence mixers

candidates.name: j5-upper4
candidates.goal: Probe whether later layers are actually the better place to save attention cost.
candidates.hypothesis: Upper layers may be more replaceable than expected if attention's most critical role is in early representation building.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether upper-layer replacement is the cleaner placement.
candidates.why_worthy: This closes the placement map and makes the family interpretable even if all five lose.
candidates.description: replace the upper four attention layers with sequence mixers
